summary: In an open letter to their newborn daughter, Max, Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan announce the creation of the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative. They outline their philanthropic philosophy, which focuses on making long-term investments in technology, personalized learning, disease prevention, and community building to advance human potential and promote equality for future generations.

principles.statement: We have a moral responsibility to invest in the lives of future generations.
principles.rationale: Because all lives have equal value, society has an obligation to improve the lives of the many more people who will live in the future, not just those already here.
principles.source_quote: We believe all lives have equal value, and that includes the many more people who will live in future generations than live today. Our society has an obligation to invest now to improve the lives of all those coming into this world, not just those already here.

principles.statement: We must build technology to make change.
principles.rationale: While many institutions invest money into challenges, the most significant progress comes from productivity gains driven by innovation.
principles.source_quote: We must build technology to make change. Many institutions invest money in these challenges, but most progress comes from productivity gains through innovation.

principles.statement: We must back the strongest and most independent leaders in each field.
principles.rationale: Partnering with domain experts is a more effective way to achieve the mission than trying to lead the efforts yourself.
principles.source_quote: We must back the strongest and most independent leaders in each field. Partnering with experts is more effective for the mission than trying to lead efforts ourselves.

principles.statement: We must participate in policy and advocacy to shape debates.
principles.rationale: For progress to be sustainable, it must be supported by broader movements, even if many institutions are unwilling to engage in policy.
principles.source_quote: We must participate in policy and advocacy to shape debates. Many institutions are unwilling to do this, but progress must be supported by movements to be sustainable.

principles.statement: Advancing human potential and promoting equality require building inclusive and healthy communities first.
principles.rationale: Children cannot reach their full potential if they are hungry, sick, or fear for their safety due to systemic inequities.
principles.source_quote: If we want either, we must first build inclusive and healthy communities.

frameworks.name: Approach to Advancing Human Potential and Promoting Equality
frameworks.when_to_apply: When tackling massive, generational societal challenges like curing disease, improving education, or connecting the world.
steps: Make long term investments over 25, 50 or even 100 years.
Engage directly with the people you serve to understand their needs.
Build technology to drive productivity gains and innovation.
Participate in policy and advocacy to make progress sustainable.
Back the strongest and most independent leaders in each field.
Take risks today to learn lessons for tomorrow.
frameworks.source_quote: This mission — advancing human potential and promoting equality — will require a new approach for all working towards these goals.

mental_models.name: Planting Seeds (Compounding Benefits)
mental_models.description: Viewing societal investments as seeds that may show little visible progress in the short term (5-10 years) but will compound over decades to create massive transformations.
mental_models.example: Investing heavily in basic science and disease research today so that in 100 years, future generations live in a world without suffering from disease.

mental_models.name: Interconnected Inequities
mental_models.description: The lens that societal issues like health, education, poverty, and justice are not isolated problems but a connected web that must be treated holistically.
mental_models.example: Recognizing that a child cannot benefit from better educational technology if they are suffering from malnutrition, trauma, or fear of violence.

heuristics: Start at a young age to see compounding benefits throughout your life.
Partner with experts rather than trying to lead efforts yourself.
Take risks today to learn lessons for tomorrow.

signature_quotes.text: We believe all lives have equal value, and that includes the many more people who will live in future generations than live today.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the moral obligation to invest in the future.

signature_quotes.text: The greatest challenges require very long time horizons and cannot be solved by short term thinking.
signature_quotes.context: Outlining the need for 25, 50, or 100-year investments.

signature_quotes.text: Today we spend about 50 times more as a society treating people who are sick than we invest in research so you won’t get sick in the first place.
signature_quotes.context: Highlighting the misallocation of societal resources regarding disease.

signature_quotes.text: We will give 99% of our Facebook shares — currently about $45 billion — during our lives to advance this mission.
signature_quotes.context: Announcing the funding of the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.

anti_patterns.description: Trying to solve massive societal challenges with short-term thinking.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: The greatest challenges require very long time horizons (25 to 100 years) and cannot be resolved quickly.

anti_patterns.description: Spending vastly more resources on treating sick people than on researching how to prevent sickness in the first place.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to direct resources at the biggest opportunities to prevent future generations from suffering.

anti_patterns.description: One-size-fits-all education where everyone learns the same things at the same pace.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It ignores individual interests and needs, robbing society of the potential many have to offer.

anti_patterns.description: Trying to empower people without engaging directly with them.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: You cannot effectively empower people if you don't understand the actual needs and desires of their communities.
